Secondary 4 Express  ·  St Patrick's School  ·  Singapore

Four years of secondary school. Three years of competitive football. Fifty-six hours of community service. One conviction: that leadership, discipline, and genuine care for others are the foundations I want to build a career in business on.

What I've done

Achievements & Contributions

Four years of secondary school, across the pitch, the classroom, and the community. This is what I've built.

2024 — Secondary 2
Co-Captain, C Division — NSG Top 8
Being appointed co-captain in Secondary 2 was a responsibility I took seriously. It demanded more than wearing the armband — I had to bring the right attitude to every training session, communicate clearly with my coach, and hold the team together through difficult fixtures. We finished Top 8 in the National School Games that year, an achievement I remain genuinely proud of. I also had the privilege of representing the school on our overseas football immersion trip to Bangkok, an experience that broadened my perspective considerably.
Co-CaptainNSG Top 8Exco MemberBangkok 20245th Place League
2024 — Secondary 2
Father-Son Football Fiesta — Champions
Winning the Father-Son Football Fiesta alongside my father remains one of my most meaningful memories from secondary school. Football has always been something we share, so achieving victory together carried significance well beyond the result itself.
Tournament Champions2024
2025 — Secondary 3
B Division NSG — 5th Place & Upper Sec Exco
Progressing to B Division meant competing against older and more physically developed opponents, which pushed me to grow considerably as both a player and a leader. We secured 5th place in the Boys League 1 — a strong result for our squad. I continued serving on the Exco, taking on greater responsibility for how the CCA was run, and returned to Bangkok for a second football immersion trip.
B Division NSG5th Place LeagueUpper Sec ExcoBangkok 2025
2026 — Secondary 4
Starting Centre-Back — NSG 2026
Earning the starting centre-back position in my final year is the culmination of four years of dedicated effort. The role demands constant anticipation of the game, organisation of the players around me, and decisive judgement under pressure. It has taught me, above all, that effective leadership does not require being the loudest voice on the pitch. Three years in NSG. Three years on the Exco. I committed fully to this team, and I believe it shows.
Starting Centre-BackNSG 2026Exco (3rd Year)
2023–2026 — Full Tenure
CCA Exco — 3 Years & Leadership Development
Serving on the Exco for three consecutive years gave me genuine influence over how our CCA operated. From coordinating training schedules to mentoring younger players, this was a substantive leadership role rather than a nominal title. I also completed both Introduction to Leadership modules (2023 and 2024) through the school's formal programme, which gave me the vocabulary to articulate leadership principles I had already been practising in the field.
Exco 2024Exco 2025Exco 2026Leadership Modules 1 & 2

Edusave Good Progress Award
Presented by the Ministry of Education to Secondary 2 students at St Patrick's School who demonstrated significant improvement in academic performance alongside good conduct. Receiving this award reinforced the value of perseverance and showed me that consistent effort and discipline lead to meaningful results.
2024  ·  Ministry of Education

Academic Merit Award — Art Excellence
Awarded for achieving the highest mark in the level for Art in Secondary 2. I take art seriously — not merely as a technical skill, but as a means of expressing something authentic. Achieving the top mark in my cohort was a genuinely proud moment.
2 January 2025  ·  St Patrick's School

Brother Joseph McNally Book Prize
This is one of the school's most meaningful prizes, awarded to students who embody strong values and make a tangible contribution to the school community. Being recognised in this way, in my final years at SPS, carries considerable significance for me.
12 April 2025  ·  St Patrick's School
📄 View Certificate
Click to view 🎓
ApLM Certificate — Understanding Online Business
I attended Temasek Polytechnic's Applied Learning Module in June 2025 and completed it with a Pass. Beyond the theoretical content, we were given the opportunity to build our own websites in groups — my team developed a streetwear clothing advertising site. The experience confirmed for me that TP is the right environment to pursue my education.
June 2025  ·  Temasek Polytechnic
📄 View Certificate
Click to view 🏅
CCA Certificate — Excellent Attainment
I received an overall EXCELLENT CCA grade, the highest classification attainable — Level 5 in both Leadership and Service, Level 4 in Participation, and 2 Bonus Points. This certificate stands as a complete record of my contributions outside the classroom across the entirety of secondary school.
2026  ·  St Patrick's School

Funniest & Sportiest Classmate
My classmates voted me both the Funniest and Sportiest in class. While informal, these recognitions meant a great deal to me because they reflected how my peers saw me — as someone who contributes positively, works well with others and helps build a supportive team culture.

2023 — Secondary 1
Sec 1 Love in Action — 13 Hours
My first VIA project was with LTA & SMRT, distributing tissue packets to commuters at MRT stations. It was a modest gesture, but approaching strangers with simple kindness taught me how meaningfully such small acts can affect someone's day.
LTA & SMRT13 Hours2023
2024 — Secondary 2
Sec 2 Love in Action & SPS Football Gives Back — 22 Hours
Secondary 2 involved two VIA projects. The first, with NParks & Sndy SG, saw us spend 14 hours at East Coast Park planting trees, clearing beach litter, and removing invasive vines and parasitic plants from the bushes. The second, SPS Football Gives Back in partnership with Food from the Heart, had our football team preparing and distributing food to families in need — a particularly meaningful initiative given what football means to all of us.
NParksFood from the Heart22 Hours2024
2025 — Secondary 3
Sec 3 Love in Action — 13 Hours
This year we partnered with Montfort Care & Fei Yue Community Services, visiting elderly residents and helping clean their homes. It was the most personally significant VIA experience I have undertaken — simply sitting and conversing with someone who receives few visitors reminded me that community service is not measured in hours, but in genuinely showing up for people.
Montfort CareFei Yue13 Hours2025
2026 — Secondary 4
Church Ministry — 8 Hours (Self-Initiated)
In Secondary 4, I independently organised my own VIA through my church — not because it was required, but because I believed that meaningful service should come from personal conviction rather than obligation. Taking ownership of this experience reflects the values I strive to live by and the initiative I hope to bring to everything I do.
Self-InitiatedChurch Ministry8 Hours2026
2023–2025
Physical Enrichment & School Events
Beyond football, I committed myself to much of what SPS had to offer — completing the 9km Endurance Walk four times, Sport Climbing Level 1, 1-Star Kayaking, SPS Harmony Games, Sec 1 to 3 Level Camps, Interclass Games in every year, National Day Dance, and the SPS Cycling Programme. I have always believed in showing up and committing fully, regardless of the activity.
9km Endurance Walk x4ClimbingKayakingLevel Camps x3
Creative work

Featured on SPS Artworks

I was featured twice on @sps_artworks, the school's official art Instagram page, in Secondary 2. Both pieces meant something personal to me — one was for my late grandfather, the other was for National Day. Click either card to see it up close.

Community service

56 Hours of VIA

I didn't do VIA just to hit the hours. Every project taught me something — about patience, about other people, and about what it actually means to care about your community. The Level 5 Attainment is a reflection of that.

Why business

Why Temasek Polytechnic?

“The skills I've built on the pitch — reading situations, leading people, making quick decisions — are exactly what business runs on.”

Business appeals to me because it brings together the qualities I am naturally drawn to — leading people, solving problems, and identifying the optimal course of action under pressure. As a football captain, I was never merely playing the game; I was reading it, adapting strategy in real time, and carrying my teammates with me. This is not far removed from what effective business leadership demands.

The ApLM programme at TP in June 2025 gave me a concrete sense of what studying here would entail. The learning extended well beyond theory into genuine practical application — my group built a streetwear clothing advertising website entirely from scratch: goodboyzonly.my.canva.site. This hands-on approach to learning is precisely what I am seeking. I am applying to the School of Business because I am confident TP will equip me with the tools to convert the instincts I have developed over four years into tangible, real-world capability.
